rationale: In the theory development, the citation is used to justify the central process-versus-outcome distinction that underpins the paper's mechanism, not just to provide generic background. The authors explicitly state that their learning-mindset construct builds on this literature and applies the process/outcome idea to education consumption. They also return to the cited paper in the discussion when arguing broader implications for service contexts, reinforcing its framing role.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Yang, Mao, and Peracchio (2012) as part of the core conceptual base showing that consumers can focus on process versus outcome in consumption evaluation. Tu et al. rely on that foundation to construct process/outcome learning mindsets in education marketing.
